_comment: FROZEN TCB test vectors (SPEC §3.1). These lock the wapps-secrets v1 on-wire formats. The CLI (Go) and the secrets-gate Worker (TypeScript) MUST both reproduce these byte-for-byte; any divergence is a release-blocking defect. Regenerate ONLY with an explicit versioned format change + migration plan.

wrap._comment: Deterministic X25519 DEK wrap (§3.5.5). dek = bytes 0..31; recipient identity from scalar 0x42*32; slot below. Output is a complete single-recipient age v1 file, decryptable by standard age.

blob._comment: v1 blob container (§3.5.4): WSB1 + nonce(24) + XChaCha20-Poly1305(padded, AAD=slot). dek = bytes 0..31; nonce = 0x11*24; padded to 256B bucket.

ed25519._comment: Ed25519 signature envelope (§3.6.2): sig = Ed25519.Sign(sk, SHA-256(msg)). key_id = sha256 of the 32-byte pubkey.

ed25519_negatives._comment: NEGATIVE ed25519 cross-vectors (release-blocking). Each case MUST be REJECTED by BOTH the Go verifier (crypto/ed25519, RFC8032 cofactorless, S<L) AND the TS Worker verifier (verifyRaw → @noble ed25519.verify with {zip215:false}). accepted_without_fix=true means @noble's DEFAULT (zip215:true, cofactored) would ACCEPT it → proves the zip215:false fix in verify.ts is load-bearing (a party influencing sig bytes under a trusted key could otherwise craft a sig the Worker ACCEPTS but every Go CLI REJECTS). message is hashed (D=SHA-256(msg)) by the verifier before ed25519 verify, exactly like the positive ed25519 vector. Generation: S+L overflow from a real sig (s_ge_l); the standard ed25519 low-order point set with S=0 forgeries (small-order + one non-canonical x-sign encoding). VECTOR-GENERATION GAP: (1) @noble 1.9.7 enforces S<L in BOTH zip215 modes, so the S>=L vector is parity-only (accepted_without_fix=false), not a cofactor-divergence proof; the small-order/non-canonical S=0 vectors carry that proof. (2) Go's crypto/ed25519 is permissive on SOME degenerate encodings — a non-canonical *identity* pubkey (eeff…7f) and non-canonical y=P order-4 (edff…7f) are ACCEPTED by Go with an all-zero-ish sig, so those are NOT valid reject-on-both vectors and are excluded; we use fc85 (non-canonical order-8, x-sign bit) with R=0-point which Go rejects. (3) A non-canonical R inside an otherwise-valid full-order signature can't be constructed deterministically without a real malleable sig, so R non-canonicality is covered only via these low-order cases.

cases.name: s_ge_l
cases.reason: Malleable signature (R, S+L): S is non-canonical (S >= group order L). RFC8032 requires S<L; @noble rejects S>=L in BOTH zip215 modes, so accepted_without_fix=false (this vector documents S>=L parity, not the cofactor divergence).

cases.name: small_order_8_pubkey
cases.reason: Public key A is a small-order (order-8) point; sig = (R=A, S=0). Cofactored (zip215:true) verify: [8*0]B == [8]R + [8k]A reduces to O==O → FORGERY ACCEPTED for any message. Cofactorless (Go / zip215:false) rejects.

cases.name: small_order_4_pubkey
cases.reason: Public key A is the order-4 point (y=0); sig = (R=0-point, S=0). Same cofactor forgery: zip215:true accepts, Go / zip215:false reject.

cases.name: noncanonical_order_8_pubkey
cases.reason: Public key A is a NON-CANONICAL order-8 encoding (x-sign bit set: fc85, same y-coordinate as the canonical fc05 point); sig = (R=0-point, S=0). zip215:true decodes the non-canonical A (y>=P permitted) and cofactor-forges → accept; Go / zip215:false reject the non-canonical/low-order forgery.

shamir._comment: Shamir 2-of-3 over GF(2^8) (§3.9). secret = 0x42*32; deterministic rng = repeating 0xAB,0xCD; x-coords fixed 1,2,3 (last byte of each share).
